No, Paul McCartney isn't available to sign autographs. Or Bob Dylan. Or Simon & Garfunkel. But you can see a video of the Beatles' performance at Hollywood Bowl on August 23, 1964, regarded as one of the most famous events in rock and roll history. You can see it all at the Hollywood Bowl Museum. Located at 2301 North Highland Avenue, at the bottom of Peppertree Lane, this multi-level facility chronicles the history of Hollywood Bowl since its opening in 1922. The main floor features the permanent exhibition called Hollywood Bowl: Music for Everyone. Relive performances by Billie Holliday and Ella Fitzgerald and Fleetwood Mac and the Doors and Jimi Hendrix and Igor Stravinsky and Leonard Bernstein. The second floor features rotating exhibitions related to specific eras and events in the history of Hollywood Bowl. Like the 1958 day-long teeny-bopper with Duane Eddy, Bobby Darin and the Champs, the Summer Spectacular on July 3, 1965 with the Beach Boys, Sonny & Cher, the Byrds and the Righteous Brothers, Bob Dylan and the Band on September 3, 1965 and Simon & Garfunkel on August 23, 1968. It surely makes for a memorable evening under the stars.
